morning_pages.notes: Three sides. Mostly circling the question of what Aliveness is actually about. I wrote: 'The problem isn't that people are unalive. The problem is that they are braced against life rather than open to it.' I don't know if that's the book yet but it's something.

writing.main_project: Exploratory notes toward Aliveness — not drafting yet
writing.notes: Used the 3/3/3. Two and a half hours on the new thing, then newsletter reply, then admin. Felt right. The discipline of stopping is still the hardest part.

imperfectionist.status: Next issue drafted — on the paradox of trying to be more spontaneous
imperfectionist.notes: I like it. The irony of the topic is the point, not a problem to solve.

family.notes: Rowan lost a tooth. He spent the entire walk home explaining the tooth fairy economy to me with more seriousness than most adults bring to actual economics.
family.wife: She had a good writing day. Said the chapter she's been stuck on finally moved. I made dinner.

atelic_activity: Walked to the edge of the moors after school run. No phone. An hour. Couldn't tell you what I thought about.
reading: Heidegger — Being and Time, the section on thrownness. Still essential.
blockers: Aliveness argument still blurry — need more exploratory time before committing to a structure
note: The flatness today isn't bad. It's winter in Yorkshire. Flatness is appropriate.
